metadata.notes: Synthetic regulatory/tributário-style case. Level-2 composition: temporal cutover plus production-data escopo.
metadata.translation_notes: Tradução preliminar em português do Brasil para revisão humana; IDs, URLs, tags e estrutura foram preservados.

representations.raw:
Synthetic Data Incident Reporting Rulebook.

Seção 3. Covered Incident Reporting. Covered processors não deveify the Data Oversight Authority within seventy-two hours after confirming a reportable personal-data breach. The seventy-two-hour period begins at the time the processor confirms that production personal data was accessed or disclosed without authorization.

Seção 4. Sandbox and Masked Data. Incidents limited entirely to masked sandbox data are not reportable to the Data Oversight Authority under Seção 3. Such incidentes deve still be recorded in the internal incidente log within five business days. This exclusion does not apply if any production personal data was involved.

Seção 7. Small-Processor Pilot Cutover. Until 31 December 2025, processors enrolled in the small-processor pilot could report covered incidentes within ten business days after confirmation. Effective 1 January 2026, the pilot reporting window ended, and all covered processors use the seventy-two-hour reporting window in Seção 3. The applicable reporting window is determined by the incidente confirmation date, not by the date the investigation began.
representations.annotated:
Synthetic Data Incident Reporting Rulebook.

<rule id="R1">Seção 3. Covered Incident Reporting. Covered processors não deveify the Data Oversight Authority within seventy-two hours after confirming a reportable personal-data breach. The seventy-two-hour period begins at the time the processor confirms that production personal data was accessed or disclosed without authorization.</rule>

<scope id="S1" bounds="R1" condition="production personal data involved">Seção 4. Sandbox and Masked Data. Incidents limited entirely to masked sandbox data are not reportable to the Data Oversight Authority under Seção 3. Such incidentes deve still be recorded in the internal incidente log within five business days. This exclusion does not apply if any production personal data was involved.</scope>

<temporal_dependency id="T1" governs="reporting_window" cutover_date="2026-01-01" anchor="incident_confirmation_date" retroactive="false">Seção 7. Small-Processor Pilot Cutover. Until 31 December 2025, processors enrolled in the small-processor pilot could report covered incidentes within ten business days after confirmation. Effective 1 January 2026, the pilot reporting window ended, and all covered processors use the seventy-two-hour reporting window in Seção 3. The applicable reporting window is determined by the incidente confirmation date, not by the date the investigation began.</temporal_dependency>
representations.compiled:
regras:
  - id: R1
    name: covered_incidente_reporting
    applies_to: covered_processors
    trigger: confirmed_reportable_personal_data_breach
    authority: Data Oversight Authority
    reporting_window: 72_hours
    window_start: confirmation_time
escopos:
  - id: S1
    bounds: R1
    reportable_when:
      production_personal_data_involved: true
    excluded_when:
      data_environment: masked_sandbox_only
    excluded_incidente_exigement:
      internal_incidente_log_window: 5_business_days
    exclusion_not_available_if:
      production_personal_data_involved: true
temporal_dependencies:
  - id: T1
    governs: reporting_window
    anchor: incidente_confirmation_date
    before_or_on: 2025-12-31
    small_processor_pilot_window: 10_business_days
    on_or_after: 2026-01-01
    all_processors_window: 72_hours
    investigation_start_date_irrelevant: true
    priority: date_anchor_controls_window

query: A processor enrolled in the small-processor pilot began investigating suspicious access on 29 December 2025. On 2 January 2026 at 10:00, it confirmed that production customer email addresses had been disclosed without authorization. Qual/O que reporting window applies?

gold_answer.full: The processor não deveify the Data Oversight Authority within seventy-two hours after confirmation on 2 January 2026 at 10:00. The incidente involves production personal data, so the sandbox exclusion does not apply. Embora the investigation began in December and the processor was in the small-processor pilot, Section 7 anchors the reporting window to the confirmation date. Because confirmation occurred on or after 1 January 2026, the pilot ten-business-day window no longer applies.
gold_answer.minimal: Seventy-two hours from confirmation on 2 January 2026 at 10:00; the ten-business-day pilot window does not apply.
required_relations: S1 determines that production personal data makes the incidente reportable under R1
T1 selects the post-cutover seventy-two-hour window based on confirmation date, not investigation start date

local_probes.probe: Under Section 3, when does the seventy-two-hour reporting period begin?
local_probes.expected_answer: It begins when the processor confirms that production personal data was accessed or disclosed without authorization.

local_probes.probe: Are incidentes limited entirely to masked sandbox data reportable to the Data Oversight Authority?
local_probes.expected_answer: No. They are not reportable to the Authority but deve be recorded internally within five business days.

local_probes.probe: Qual/O que date determines whether the small-processor pilot window or the seventy-two-hour window applies?
local_probes.expected_answer: The incidente confirmation date determines the applicable window, not the investigation start date.

composition_hallucination: The processor has ten business days porque the investigation began before 1 January 2026 and it was enrolled in the pilot.
No Authority notice is exigido porque small processors are excluded after the cutover.
wrong_escalation: The processor não deveify within one hour porque any post-cutover breach is automatically critical.
The processor não deveify a court rather than the Data Oversight Authority.
refusal: The regrabook does not say whether the investigation start date or confirmation date controls.
failure_examples.retrieval_failure_note: If Section 7 is absent, choosing a reporting window is a retrieval failure. If Section 4 is absent, determining reportability is incomplete.

controls.negative_control_query: A small-processor pilot participant confirmed on 30 December 2025 that production personal data had been disclosed without authorization. Qual/O que reporting window applies?
controls.negative_control_gold: Ten business days after confirmation. The incidente is reportable production data, and the confirmation date falls before the 1 January 2026 cutover.
